claims.statement: Document consolidated from GM_research and carlink_native sources
Last updated 2026-02-19
CarPlay audio protocol uses message type 0x07 (AudioData)
Audio messages use two key identifiers: decode_type and audio_type
decode_type determines audio format and quality (playback vs voice)
audio_type determines audio channel and routing
Magic field is at offset 0x00, 4 bytes, value 0x55AA55AA (little-endian)
PayloadLen field is at offset 0x04, 4 bytes, indicates bytes after the 16-byte header
MsgType field is at offset 0x08, 4 bytes, value 7 for AudioData
Checksum field is at offset 0x0C, 4 bytes, calculated as MsgType XOR 0xFFFFFFFF equals -8
Audio header begins at offset 0x10 and is 12 bytes in size
decode_type field is at offset 0x10, 4 bytes
volume field is at offset 0x14, 4 bytes, float volume multiplier 0.0-1.0
audio_type field is at offset 0x18, 4 bytes, stream type 1=main, 2=nav, 3=mic
Payload starts at offset 0x1C, contains PCM samples or 1-byte command
claims.quote: │ 0x1C+   │  N   │ Data       │ PCM samples OR 1-byte command         │

claims.statement: Total header size is 28 bytes (0x1C)
PayloadLen equals 12 (audio header) plus audio_payload_size

The iAP2 Message Catalog documents wire-format field semantics for RouteGuidance (0x5201/0x5202) and Telephony (CallState 0x4155, Communication 0x4158).

21 distinct NaviJSON key-combinations were observed across captures.
The combination [_iap2] is a bare per-tick state ping.
The combination [NaviRemainDistance, _iap2] is a distance-changed tick.
The combination [NaviStatus, _iap2] is a status-changed tick.
The route-start packet combination is [NaviStatus, NaviTimeToDestination, NaviDestinationName, NaviDistanceToDestination, NaviAPPName, _iap2].
The combination [NaviManeuverType, NaviOrderType] is a bare maneuver burst.
The combination [NaviRoadName, NaviOrderType, NaviRoundaboutExit, NaviManeuverType] is a roundabout burst.
The combination [NaviRoadName, NaviOrderType, NaviTurnAngle, NaviTurnSide, NaviManeuverType] is a full turn burst.
NaviStatus is a float-to-int key mirroring 0x5201 field 0x0001 RouteGuidanceState.
NaviAPPName is a string key mirroring 0x5201 extension field 0x0013.
NaviDestinationName is a string key mirroring 0x5201 extension field 0x0004.
NaviRemainDistance is a float-to-int meters key mirroring 0x5201 field 0x000a DistanceToNextManeuver.
NaviTimeToDestination is a float-to-int seconds key mirroring 0x5201 field 0x0006 TimeRemainingToDestination.
NaviDistanceToDestination is a float-to-int meters key mirroring 0x5201 field 0x0007 DistanceRemaining.
NaviManeuverType is an int CPManeuverType key mirroring 0x5202 field 0x0003.
NaviRoadName mirrors 0x5202 field 0x0002 (instruction_text), NOT 0x0004 (post_road).
NaviTurnSide is an int 0x5202 driving-side derivative.
NaviTurnAngle is an int enum (not degrees) derived from 0x5202.
NaviRoundaboutExit is an int in range 1-19 derived from 0x5202.
NaviOrderType is an int from Apple's burst-sequence enum (16=roundabout, 6=turn, others) and is under-utilized by the host app.
_iap2 is a hex string carrying the raw 0x5201 frame, parsed by Iap2StateParser since versionCode 136.
claims.quote: raw 0x5201 frame — parsed by `Iap2StateParser` since versionCode 136

claims.statement: _iap2m is a hex string carrying the raw 0x5202 burst, parsed by Iap2RouteParser.
The wire format is sync 0x4040, length BE 2 bytes, type BE 2 bytes, KVPs, then a 1-byte trailer.
Each KVP is entry_len BE 2 bytes, field_id BE 2 bytes, then value of entry_len-4 bytes.
len_be equals total frame bytes minus 1 (excludes one sync byte), and the trailer is one zero byte.
claims.quote: `len_be` = total frame bytes − 1 (excludes one sync byte). Trailer is one zero byte

claims.statement: Multiple 0x5202 frames in a single _iap2m are concatenated and the parser walks them via sync-search until the buffer is exhausted.
claims.quote: simply concatenated; parser walks them via sync-search until the buffer is exhausted

claims.statement: 0x5201 RouteGuidanceUpdate has 21 fields sent per-tick at about 1Hz.
0x5201 field 0x0000 is a u16 BE header marker always equal to 0x002a, the first parameter or set start.
0x5201 field 0x0001 RouteGuidanceState is a u8 enum with values 0/1/2/6, likely inactive/active/paused.
0x5201 field 0x0002 ManeuverState is a u8 enum with values 1/2/3 representing internal maneuver lifecycle.
0x5201 field 0x0003 CurrentRoadName is a UTF-8 road the user is physically on now, distinct from NaviRoadName.
claims.quote: Road user is PHYSICALLY ON now. Distinct from `NaviRoadName`

claims.statement: 0x5201 field 0x0004 is a post-2017 DestinationName extension (UTF-8) mirroring NaviDestinationName.
0x5201 field 0x0005 EstimatedTimeOfArrival is a u64 BE of 8 bytes, Unix seconds at arrival.
0x5201 field 0x0006 TimeRemainingToDestination is a u64 BE in seconds, mirroring NaviTimeToDestination.
0x5201 field 0x0007 DistanceRemaining is a u32 BE of 4 bytes in meters to destination, mirroring NaviDistanceToDestination.
0x5201 field 0x0008 DistanceRemainingDisplayStr is UTF-8 such as '1.7' or '350'.
0x5201 field 0x0009 DistanceRemainingDisplayUnits is a u8 enum where 1=mi/km and 4=ft/m.
0x5201 field 0x000a DistanceToNextManeuver is a u32 BE in meters to next turn, mirroring NaviRemainDistance.
0x5201 field 0x000b DistanceToNextManeuverDisplayStr is UTF-8 of 2-4 bytes.
0x5201 field 0x000c DistanceToNextManeuverDisplayUnits is a u8 enum with values 1/2/4.
0x5201 field 0x000d RouteGuidanceManeuverCurrentList is packed u16 BE acting as a CURSOR; len=4 gives (currentIdx, nextIdx) and len=2 gives (nextIdx) only at depart/arrival.
claims.quote: **CURSOR.** len=4 → `(currentIdx, nextIdx)` pair. len=2 → `(nextIdx)` only at depart/arrival

All USB messages use a common 16-byte header.
claims.quote: All USB messages use a common 16-byte header

claims.statement: The header magic field is 4 bytes at offset 0 and is 0x55AA55AA cleartext or 0x55BB55BB AES-encrypted.
The header length field is 4 bytes at offset 4 giving payload size in bytes little-endian.
The header type field is 4 bytes at offset 8 giving the message type ID little-endian.
The header type_check field is 4 bytes at offset 12 equal to type XOR 0xFFFFFFFF.
The payload begins at offset 16 and is N bytes of message-specific data.
Magic must equal 0x55AA55AA or 0x55BB55BB.
The type check must equal type XOR 0xFFFFFFFF.
Length must be less than or equal to 1048576 bytes.
Total message size equals 16 plus payload_length.
Magic 0x55AA55AA means cleartext payload and is default, always used for types 0x06, 0x07, 0x2A, 0x2C.
Magic 0x55BB55BB means AES-CBC encrypted payload, used for all other types when encryption is enabled via CMD_ENABLE_CRYPT.
claims.quote: All other types when encryption is enabled via CMD_ENABLE_CRYPT

claims.statement: Assembly proof at fcn.000645ec (0x645f4) uses add.w r2, r2, 0x110011 to transform 0x55AA55AA into 0x55BB55BB.

Phase 0 means Session Terminated / Idle, VERIFIED, used for session end detection.
Phase 4 means Waiting for WiFi Hotspot for wireless CarPlay/AA.
Phase 5 means Waiting for AirPlay session establishment.
Phase 7 means Connecting / Negotiating, VERIFIED, at 4.498s payload 07 00 00 00.
Phase 8 means Streaming Ready / Active, VERIFIED, at 7.504s payload 08 00 00 00.
Phase 11 means CarLife Download prompt (Baidu CarLife protocol).
Phase 13 means Session Negotiation Failed, VERIFIED, typically a viewArea/safeArea constraint violation triggering a Phase 7 to 13 reconnect loop.
Phase 1001 means Hardware Error Bluetooth (RTL HCI init or hfpd crash).
Phase 1002 means Hardware Error WiFi (RTL8822CS driver load or hostapd crash).
Phases 1003 and 1004 mean unclassified Hardware Error Unknown.
The host sees only phases 0, 7, and 8 over USB while internal phases drive the adapter state machine.
claims.quote: The host sees only phases 0, 7, and 8 over USB

claims.statement: Internal Phase 0 DISCONNECTED kills AppleCarPlay/ARMiPhoneIAP2 and cleans up.
Internal Phase 1 CONNECTED sets state=3 and prepares the session.
Internal Phase 2 SCREEN_INFO sends Phase(type=3, value=7) to host.
claims.quote: 2 | SCREEN_INFO | Send Phase(type=3, value=7) to host

claims.statement: Internal Phase 3 STREAMING sends Phase(type=3, value=8) to host and starts streaming.
claims.quote: 3 | STREAMING | Send Phase(type=3, value=8) to host, start streaming

claims.statement: Internal Phase 100 WIRELESS_PROBE does HNP check for wired and screen info relay.
Internal Phase 101 USB_HNP_OK logs PHASE_USBHNPOK! and launches the AppleCarPlay binary.
Internal Phase 102 KILL kills AppleCarPlay and resets the session.
Internal Phase 103 UI_CALLBACK is the UI callback handler.
Internal Phase 104 RESET_WIFI logs PHASE_ResetWiFi and restarts WiFi.
Internal Phase 105 PHONE_CALL_START does phone call audio routing setup.
claims.quote: 105 | PHONE_CALL_START | Phone call audio routing setup

claims.statement: Internal Phase 106 PHONE_CALL_END ends phone call audio routing.
claims.quote: 106 | PHONE_CALL_END | End phone call audio routing

claims.statement: Internal Phase 200 AUTO_CONNECT_TRIGGER triggers the auto-connect sequence.
For session termination use Unplugged (0x04) or Phase 0, and do not rely on Command 1010.
claims.quote: Do NOT rely on Command 1010 (DeviceWifiNotConnected) for session end

claims.statement: In the verified init sequence, at 0.132s the host sends SendFile setting /tmp/screen_dpi=240.
At 0.253s the host sends Open at 2400x960@60fps format=5.
At 4.180s the adapter sends Plugged with phoneType=3 and 0x04=1.
At 4.498s the adapter sends Phase 7 (connecting) and at 7.504s Phase 8 (streaming ready).

claims.statement: AutoKit init step 1 H0() sends 0xA0 minimal PID JSON for app identification first pass.

AutoKit init step 5 X0() sends 0x01 Open and the adapter echoes Open plus info.
AutoKit init step 7 i0() sends 0xA0 full AppInfo JSON, the second-pass app identification.
All AutoKit messages after step 2 are encrypted with magic 0x55BB55BB except exempt types 0x06, 0x07, 0x2A, 0x2C.

claims.statement: Type 11 (0x0B) CMD_CARPLAY_MODE_CHANGE is A to H only, ACTIVE, forwarded from the iPhone CarPlay stack.
Type 16 (0x10) CMD_CARPLAY_AirPlayModeChanges is A to H only, ACTIVE, forwarded from iPhone AirPlay.
Type 17 (0x11) AutoConnect_By_BluetoothAddress is BOTH, ACTIVE, an auto-reconnect trigger carrying a BT MAC string.
Type 19 (0x13) CMD_BLUETOOTH_ONLINE_LIST is BOTH, ACTIVE, BT device enumeration.
Type 27 (0x1B) BTAudioDevice_Signal is H to A and is DEAD CODE, logged and discarded with no handler.
Type 30 (0x1E) is DUAL: Bluetooth_Search inbound and BroadCastRemoteCxCy outbound (28B resolution), ACTIVE outbound NO-OP inbound.
Type 119 (0x77) FactorySetting is BOTH: A to H empty idle notification, H to A 4B factory reset, ACTIVE dual-purpose.
Type 136 (0x88) CMD_DEBUG_TEST is H to A with 4B subcommand, ACTIVE, log capture/retrieval.
Type 160 (0xA0) CMD_APP_INFO is HOST-ONLY, not in firmware dispatch, sent twice (PID only before Open, full after Open).
Type 161 (0xA1) ICCOA Open/Info is A to H 12-24B, ACTIVE for ICCOA protocol only.
Type 162 (0xA2) CMD_BOX_CONFIG is HOST-ONLY, AutoKit sends DayNightMode and WiFiChannel as a separate config channel.
Type 205 (0xCD) HUDComand_A_Reboot is H to A with no payload, ACTIVE full system reboot.
Type 206 (0xCE) HUDComand_A_ResetUSB is H to A with no payload, ACTIVE USB gadget reset.
Type 240 (0xF0) CMD_ENABLE_CRYPT is H to A trigger and A to H ack, ACTIVE.
Type 253 (0xFD) HUDComand_D_Ready is H to A with no payload, ACTIVE display-ready signal.
